howItWorks.intro: This triangle inequality checker uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is the triangle inequality theorem?
faq.answer: The triangle inequality theorem states that the sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the third side. All three combinations must be checked: a+b > c, a+c > b, and b+c > a.

faq.question: What is the difference between scalene, isosceles, and equilateral?
faq.answer: A scalene triangle has no equal sides, an isosceles triangle has exactly two equal sides, and an equilateral triangle has all three sides equal.

faq.question: How can I tell if a triangle is acute, right, or obtuse?
faq.answer: Sort the sides so a ≤ b ≤ c. If a² + b² = c², it is a right triangle. If a² + b² > c², it is acute. If a² + b² < c², it is obtuse.

faq.question: Can three equal lengths form a triangle?
faq.answer: Yes, three equal positive lengths always form an equilateral triangle. For example, sides 5, 5, 5 satisfy 5+5 > 5 in all combinations.
